CSCE 633 Syllabus

Section 700 (44010)

Machine Learning

Spring 2026 - College Station

End Date: 05/05/2026

Meeting Location:  ONLINE ONLINE

Credit Hours:  3

Instructor Details

Bobak Mortazavi

Email:  bobakm+csce633-700@tamu.edu

Office Location:  308 PETR

Phone:  979.458.2642

Office Hours

M 3-4 PM and By Appointment

Preferred Contact Method

e-mail

Catalog Description

Machine Learning. (3-0). Credit 3. Machine learning is the study of self-modifying

computer systems that can acquire new knowledge and improve their own

performance; survey machine learning techniques, which include induction from

examples, conceptual clustering, explanation-based learning, exemplar learning and

analogy, discovery and genetic algorithms. Prerequisite: CSCE 420 or CSCE 625.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  CSCE 420 or CSCE 625.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of the course students are expected to be able to:

formalize  the fundamental techniques and methods of machine learning: methods,

data processing, model selection, etc.

analyze and compare  the strengths and weaknesses of commonly used machine

learning approaches

evaluate    the underlying mathematical relationships within and across advanced

machine learning algorithms and the paradigms of supervised and unsupervised

learning

design, implement, and apply  various advanced machine learning algorithms in a

range of real-world applications.

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

The cutoff for an `A' will be at most 90% of total score, 80% for a `B', 70% for a `C', and 60%

for a `D'. Anything below 60% will an ‘F’.

1. Four homework assignments = 40%

2. One Final Project Assignment = 25%

3. Ten quizzes = 35%, the three lowest quiz grades will be dropped.

All assignments, projects, and quizzes are individual assignments.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

No Late Assignments will be accepted. Late assignments are any deliverables after

the established deadline.

All assignments are due at 11:59 pm CT (time in College Station, TX) on the date

listed unless otherwise specified. Always refer to the due date listed on Canvas.

Additional Course Information

1.  Computer accounts: You should ask for TAMU HPRC access for implementing the

assignments, if necessary

2.  Programming languages permitted: Python and must be executable on CS unix hosts

or other public systems in the department lab

3.  You must have a camera that can be used by Respondus Lockdown Browser for

online proctoring of quizzes.

4.  It is your responsibility to find a place to take quizzes in which the camera usage

during quizzes does not result in privacy problems.

AI Statement

The use of Generative AI tools is generally accepted in this class.

Students are expected to document how the tools were used, including

the prompts provided, and describe how they evaluated.  Engaging in the

various aspects of creative pursuits (e.g., writing, coding, drawing) is critical to

education in a broad sense. While AI technologies will continue shaping how

we approach these creative tasks, the critical work of creativity relies on

integrity, originality, and ethical conduct in regard to appropriate

representation as an author or creator. Thus, submitting work with a

significant percentage of AI-generated content, unless otherwise permitted,

can be considered academic misconduct under Texas A&M University

Student Rule 20.  Students must therefore cite the use of Generative AI

tools and document what they have contributed to an assignment.
